change_classes.read_only: No repo or runtime mutation; truth collection only.
change_classes.repo_only: Repo-tracked documentation, manifests, generators and SSOT updates only.
change_classes.reversible_runtime: Controlled runtime change with explicit rollback and post-verify.

lanes.label: Lane A: MVP Closeout
lanes.goal: Keep the internal business MVP closed and documented as the completed first delivery lane.
lanes.done_when: release-mvp-gate stays MVP_READY with no reopened manual blockers.
lanes.next_operator_action: Only intervene if a real regression reopens the internal business MVP.
lanes.next_codex_action: Treat Lane A as sealed baseline truth while newer lanes move forward.

lanes.label: Lane B: Website/Public
lanes.goal: Get a minimal public HTTPS baseline for www.frawo-tech.de so the edge is technically green even while design and content remain provisional.
lanes.done_when: frawo-tech.de and www.frawo-tech.de are externally reachable over valid HTTPS through the chosen public edge with documented rollback and no admin UI exposure, even if the website design/content is not final yet.
lanes.next_operator_action: Decide and provide the final public edge path, currently Cloudflare proxy or equivalent public IPv4/TLS path, so HTTPS can go green even before the website design is final.
lanes.next_codex_action: Keep the website origin, release gate, and rollout docs aligned around the HTTPS-baseline scope; as soon as the edge path is chosen, execute the smallest safe sequence.

lanes.label: Lane C: Security/PBS/Infra
lanes.goal: Hold the recovered platform on a professional baseline while finishing DNS, admin-path, and infrastructure follow-through.
lanes.done_when: Split-DNS is finalized, the current admin path is explicit, and infra follow-up items are tracked without hidden drift.
lanes.next_operator_action: Finalize the restricted nameserver for hs27.internal and provide the chosen admin path for wolfstudiopc.
lanes.next_codex_action: Keep SSOT, audits, and infra runbooks consistent with the recovered runtime and avoid reopening solved incidents.

lanes.label: Lane D: Stockenweiler
lanes.goal: Keep the support rollout prepared without starting live cutover work too early.
lanes.done_when: Stockenweiler is intentionally promoted into an active rollout with verified access, cert, and support path.
lanes.next_operator_action: Share real gateway, certificate, and device facts when the support rollout is intentionally resumed.
lanes.next_codex_action: Maintain the support model and inventory truth, but do not start site rollout work yet.

lanes.label: Lane E: Radio/Media
lanes.goal: Keep media stable and recover the radio backend without turning the lane into a feature-expansion track.
lanes.done_when: Jellyfin remains green and radio-node is back online behind the existing frontdoor.
lanes.next_operator_action: Perform the on-site radio-node recovery steps so the Pi is visible again on LAN or Tailnet.
lanes.next_codex_action: Keep the media path green, hold the radio truth honest, and resume integration only after the Pi is reachable again.

preflight_checks: scripts/tailscale_split_dns_readiness_check.sh returns split_dns_prereqs_ready=yes
scripts/adguard_pilot_readiness_check.sh returns adguard_pilot_ready=yes
toolbox DNS answers hs27.internal correctly on 100.82.26.53:53
tasks.rollback_plan: If restricted DNS causes client breakage, remove the restricted nameserver entry or revert the local NRPT helper and fall back to direct Tailscale frontdoor access.
tasks.goal: hs27.internal resolves cleanly for remote Tailscale clients through the recovered toolbox DNS path.
tasks.done_when: The restricted nameserver for hs27.internal points to 100.82.26.53 and remote clients resolve internal names without local hacks.
tasks.next_operator_action: Set the restricted nameserver for hs27.internal to 100.82.26.53 in Tailscale Admin or run the elevated local NRPT helper.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep the split-DNS checks and SSOT pointed at 100.82.26.53 and re-verify after the operator-side DNS action.

preflight_checks: VM220 website origin answers internally on 10.1.0.22
Public DNS still points at the intended target
No internal admin UI is included in the public scope
tasks.rollback_plan: If the public cutover fails, keep DNS and proxy on the last known-good HTTP-only state and do not publish a broken HTTPS path as released.
tasks.goal: Finish the smallest safe public HTTPS baseline for frawo-tech.de and www.frawo-tech.de.
tasks.done_when: frawo-tech.de redirects cleanly and www.frawo-tech.de serves over valid HTTPS with rollback notes and no public admin exposure, even if website design/content is still provisional.
tasks.next_operator_action: Choose and provide the final public edge path, currently Cloudflare proxy or equivalent public IPv4 routing, to get HTTPS green first.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep Lane B focused on the HTTPS-baseline outcome and do not mix it with unrelated infra or media work.

preflight_checks: Confirm radio-node is still unreachable on 192.168.2.155
Confirm radio-node is still unreachable on 100.64.23.77
Confirm toolbox frontdoor returns 502 on 100.82.26.53:8448
tasks.rollback_plan: No rollback in absent-hardware state; once the Pi is back, keep the existing reverse-proxy path and only restore known-good runtime pieces.
tasks.goal: Restore the physical radio backend so the existing frontdoor can go green again.
tasks.done_when: radio-node responds on LAN or Tailnet and the frontdoor on 100.82.26.53:8448 no longer returns 502.
tasks.next_operator_action: Check power, LEDs, boot, and LAN-link on the Pi, then bring back either the LAN or Tailscale path.
tasks.next_codex_action: Hold the diagnosis honest, then resume radio integration checks as soon as the Pi is reachable again.

preflight_checks: Confirm wolfstudiopc remains the primary admin device in SSOT
Confirm the intended admin path does not require blind full-tunnel changes
tasks.rollback_plan: If a new admin path creates unnecessary exposure, disable it and fall back to the current manual-local admin model.
tasks.goal: Restore one explicit and supportable admin path to wolfstudiopc.
tasks.done_when: OpenSSH or an equivalent documented admin path is available and reflected in SSOT.
tasks.next_operator_action: Enable the chosen admin path on wolfstudiopc, preferably OpenSSH with the existing access model.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep docs and checks ready, but avoid Windows admin-token drift until the operator chooses the exact path.

preflight_checks: Support rollout remains intentionally deferred behind current active work
Current cert and remote-access facts are collected before rollout decisions
tasks.rollback_plan: Planning-only watch state; no live site changes are made from this task.
tasks.goal: Keep Stockenweiler prepared as the next external support track without starting live cutover work prematurely.
tasks.done_when: The support model, certificate state, and access path are ready for intentional activation.
tasks.next_operator_action: Provide current facts for the expired certificate and the intended support rollout timing.
tasks.next_codex_action: Maintain the support planning truth and keep the rollout explicitly deferred.

preflight_checks: USB dongles are physically available
Vendor and product IDs are audited before passthrough
tasks.rollback_plan: If passthrough destabilizes HAOS or host USB visibility, remove the passthrough and return to the current VM state.
tasks.goal: Prepare a clean HAOS USB path without touching it prematurely.
tasks.done_when: The correct Zigbee/Z-Wave hardware is attached, identified, and documented for passthrough.
tasks.next_operator_action: Attach the intended Zigbee/Z-Wave hardware so the passthrough path can be audited.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep the guardrail documentation current and wait for real hardware before attempting runtime changes.

preflight_checks: Dedicated boot USB and datastore device are identified
Device contract is approved before datastore preparation
PBS remains out of current business-MVP scope
tasks.rollback_plan: If hardware or datastore identity is uncertain, do nothing and keep PBS in staged-not-live status.
tasks.goal: Keep the PBS rebuild path ready without risking the wrong hardware or a premature datastore action.
tasks.done_when: The guarded rebuild is executed on approved hardware and followed by a real proof backup and restore drill.
tasks.next_operator_action: Provide the approved hardware set and device serial confirmation for the guarded rebuild.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep PBS staged and documented, but do not cross the safety gate before hardware approval.

preflight_checks: Interactive apps that blocked winget are closed
The controlled workstation update runner remains the only allowed update path
tasks.rollback_plan: If an update blocks or requests elevation unexpectedly, stop the run and leave the remaining packages pending rather than forcing them.
tasks.goal: Finish the remaining GUI-gated workstation updates without reintroducing hanging update runs.
tasks.done_when: The remaining blocked-by-process and timed-out packages are cleanly resolved or explicitly deferred with a fresh audit.
tasks.next_operator_action: Close the remaining GUI apps and rerun the controlled update path when convenient.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep the hardened update runner and audit path as the only accepted workstation update method.
